assay_v1.description: ASSAY v1 — Claude/GPT-4o generated, 7 categories × 3 difficulties × 200 prompts

assay_qi_v2.description: ASSAY-QI v2.0 — quantum-augmented + retail register. 84 prompts overlap with ASSAY v1 PG-miss set (correct dedup).

tier_1_fully_public.label: Taxonomy, methodology, harness code, benign examples
tier_1_fully_public.content: arXiv paper + eval harness (Apache 2.0) + benign D0 examples
tier_1_fully_public.note: FINPROOF v1 is all-attack. Benign tier will be added in v1.1 (1,000 D0 examples).

tier_2_email_gate.label: Easy attack examples
tier_2_email_gate.rationale: Direct-difficulty examples are well-represented in public benchmarks (HarmBench, PINT). Email gate builds contact database, not restricts access.

tier_3_research.label: Medium attack examples
tier_3_research.access: FINPROOF Research Agreement (non-commercial, no training use, citation required)
tier_3_research.rationale: QCBM-generated examples (Fix1 10q, boundary 8q, gradient 8q, joint 8q) are most novel distributional contribution. Research-only to prevent training data extraction.

tier_4_withheld.label: Hard attack examples — official test set
tier_4_withheld.access: Zytra-only. Official scores at finproof.zytra.ai exclusively.
tier_4_withheld.sha256_note: Published hash allows future auditors to verify the same test set has been used across all evaluations. Inputs never released.
tier_4_withheld.rationale: Advanced ASSAY v1 prompts (multi-step authority chain) + SA Annealing boundary attacks (Semalith-targeted). Most detectable only by domain-trained models.

data_governance.pint_principle: Public and withheld splits drawn from same apparent distribution. Developer examining Tier 2+3 examples cannot reverse-engineer Tier 4 content. QCBM distributional diversity ensures this property.

semalith_miss_by_tier.note: Miss rate = p_benign >= 0.50 on ASSAY-QI v2.0 subset only
